title: xyris laxifolia var. laxifolia
intro: Xyris laxifolia var. laxifolia, also known as the False Yellow-eyed Grass, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Xyridaceae, native to the southeastern United States and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Xyris laxifolia var. laxifolia include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Xyris laxifolia var. laxifolia can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its roots and leaves can be used to make a poultice or salve for topical application.
